raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California cannot reach its emission targets without increasing hydrogen production 1,700 times the current production rate.",
    "The SB 1420 legislation aims to unlock an estimated $10 billion investment from the private sector.",
    "The SB 1420 legislation allows clean and renewable hydrogen production facilities to qualify for recent energy project permitting bills.",
    "The SB 1420 legislation would establish the world's highest hydrogen emission standard.",
    "Expanding hydrogen production is essential for California's transition to an emission-free energy and transportation system.",
    "The SB 1420 legislation requires that at least 60% of hydrogen used in transportation be renewable by 2030.",
    "By 2045, 40% of the remaining hydrogen must be a combination of 'clean' and renewable hydrogen.",
    "The definition of 'clean hydrogen' under SB 1420 is based on a process resulting in no more than 4 kilograms of CO₂e per kilogram of hydrogen."
  ]
}
```
